issues.title: Resolved: explicit-scope chat no longer triggers unrelated background session message fetches
issues.symptom: When /chat was opened with an explicit scope in the URL but without a session id, the page used to issue a background GET /sessions/:id/messages for the latest recent conversation through the global sidebar session hook.
issues.expected: Explicit-scope chat entry should only load the recent session list for shell chrome and must not fetch message history for an unrelated session unless the user explicitly opens that conversation.
issues.actual: Layout now keeps the recent-conversation rail on list-only mode, and headless browser fallback confirmed that /chat?paperId=142c2950-0a4a-4994-ba9f-28fd422b8caa no longer triggers any unrelated GET /sessions/:id/messages request.
evidence: headless_browser_2026-05-07: explicit-scope single-paper chat originally issued one unrelated GET /sessions/:id/messages request after the session list loaded
headless_browser_2026-05-07: after Layout/useSessions fix, explicit-scope single-paper chat issued zero unrelated GET /sessions/:id/messages requests
issues.notes: Visible UI had already stopped rebinding to the old session; this fix removed the remaining layout-level background message fetch by separating session-list loading from message-history loading.

issues.title: Resolved: compare mainline recovered after live API process loaded the paper-detail chunk-count fix
issues.symptom: The current compare UI initially could not be driven to a 2-paper selected state in Chrome DevTools MCP because the live `/api/v1/papers/{id}` detail path still reported evidence-ready papers as `chunkCount=0` / `evidenceReady=false`.
issues.expected: The compare selector should hydrate the same evidence-ready state from detail fetches that the list/search API already exposed, allowing two papers to be selected and compared.
issues.actual: After refreshing the live API process to load the current `PaperService.get_paper_for_api` fix, LIMA v6 and LIMA v5 both hydrated with `chunkCount=105` / `evidenceReady=true`, the compare matrix rendered, Save to Notes succeeded, and Continue in Chat entered compare-scoped chat handoff.
repro_steps: Search for LIMA and select LIMA: Less Is More for Alignment (v6) plus LIMA v5
Verify matrix render, 保存到笔记 toast, and 继续追问 navigation into compare-scoped chat
evidence: chrome_devtools_mcp_2026-05-07: page-context fetch before API refresh showed /search detail mismatch for LIMA v6/v5 readiness
chrome_devtools_mcp_2026-05-07: page-context fetch after API refresh showed /api/v1/papers/{id} returning chunkCount=105 and evidenceReady=true for LIMA v6/v5
chrome_devtools_mcp_2026-05-07: direct compare re-test rendered matrix, 跨论文洞察, 保存到笔记 toast, and compare-scoped chat handoff
issues.notes: `test_5_pages` remains an invalid compare sample in the current runtime dataset because it only has one chunk, so compare verification should use evidence-ready papers such as LIMA v6/v5.

Console warnings remained cleared after the typography-prop fix, and the current filter controls were verified to be the run-mode toggles plus baseline comparison action rather than a date-range picker.

sections.notes: Metrics, run list and detail section rendered.

issues.title: Resolved: analytics non-boolean attribute warnings
issues.symptom: Console previously showed React warnings for non-boolean attributes like font-serif and tracking-tight.
issues.expected: No React DOM attribute warnings in analytics render path.

pages.summary: Settings page loaded correctly with profile, language, display, security, API integration and diagnostics sections. Security logout flow now works from within Settings, font-size persistence was verified through reload, and localization now persists across reload after the LanguageContext storage fix.

System diagnostics and settings navigation visible.

issues.title: Resolved: settings non-boolean attribute warnings
issues.symptom: Console previously showed React warnings for non-boolean attributes like font-serif and tracking-tight.
issues.expected: No React DOM attribute warnings in settings render path.

issues.title: Resolved: settings language selection persists after reload
issues.symptom: Within Settings, switching the localization section to English previously updated the live UI but refreshing /settings returned the interface to Chinese.
issues.expected: Selected language should persist across reloads the same way font-size settings persist.
issues.actual: scholarai-language now persists as en in localStorage and the settings shell stays in English after reload.
evidence: headless_browser_2026-05-07: before reload the settings page body switched to English UI labels and wrote scholarai-language=en
headless_browser_2026-05-07: after reload the settings page remained in English while localStorage still held scholarai-language=en
issues.notes: Resolved in current working tree by persisting LanguageContext state to localStorage. The active settings subsection still resets to profile on reload, but that is a separate behavior from language persistence.

pages.summary: Global shell was re-verified under direct Chrome DevTools MCP across the authenticated workspace chrome. The user area visibly exposed the dedicated account label, settings navigation worked, logout returned to the public landing page, clicking a recent conversation reopened the real chat session, and the recent knowledge-base rail now shows normalized product-facing labels instead of seeded verification names.

sections.notes: Search route active state and section labels remain stable.
Recent conversation entry was clicked in MCP and reopened the expected real chat session URL.
Recent KB rail now shows normalized labels such as 研究资料馆 A183 in the live authenticated shell.
Authenticated PR19 E2E Dedicated User label remains visible; settings button navigated to /settings and logout returned to the public landing page.
Mobile navigation sheet opened with all workspace labels visible and routed into /search on a narrow viewport.

issues.title: Resolved: anonymous protected-route redirects no longer trigger repeated session-expired toasts
issues.symptom: When a logged-out user hit a protected route and was redirected into /login, multiple 会话已过期，请重新登录 toasts previously appeared after authentication because layout sidebar hooks had already issued unauthenticated session / knowledge-base requests.
issues.expected: Anonymous users should be redirected quietly to /login without session-expired toasts from sidebar preload hooks.
issues.actual: Layout now skips sidebar preload hooks until authentication is established, so the restored protected page no longer shows session-expired toasts after login.
repro_steps: Open a protected route such as /search?q=test_5_pages in a logged-out session
Complete login after the redirect to /login
Observe the restored page header area
evidence: headless_browser_2026-05-07: after the fix, restored /search?q=test_5_pages showed no 会话已过期，请重新登录 toast and still displayed the expected internal result card
code_fix_2026-05-07: useSessions/useKnowledgeBases both gained enabled gating and Layout only enables them for authenticated users
issues.notes: Resolved in current working tree. This remains distinct from the seeded-data issue in recent knowledge-base labels.

issues.title: Resolved: mobile navigation sheet no longer emits ref or missing-title accessibility warnings
issues.symptom: Opening the mobile navigation sheet previously emitted a React ref warning for SheetOverlay plus Radix accessibility warnings about missing DialogTitle and DialogDescription.
issues.expected: Opening the mobile navigation sheet should keep the console clean and satisfy the Dialog accessibility contract.
issues.actual: SheetOverlay and SheetContent now use forwardRef, and Layout provides hidden SheetTitle/SheetDescription so the mobile navigation sheet opens without those warnings.
evidence: headless_browser_2026-05-07: before the fix, opening the mobile navigation sheet emitted Function components cannot be given refs and missing DialogTitle/DialogDescription warnings
headless_browser_2026-05-07: after the fix, the same mobile navigation flow kept the menu usable and removed those warnings, leaving only the initial anonymous 401 network noise
issues.notes: Resolved in current working tree via sheet.tsx forwardRef alignment and hidden accessibility labels in Layout.
